communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Patrick Hayes USMS
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records reflecting the involvement of Deputy U.S. Marshal Patrick Hayes (El Paso Division) with the Chandler family of El Paso, Texas, during the period January 1, 2019 through December 31, 2019, including:

Any record of DUSM Hayes being notified by, coordinating with, or communicating with the El Paso Police Department concerning El Paso PD Incident Reports 19015111 (January 15, 2019, 10009 Buckwood Ave., El Paso — harassment complaint filed by Daniel Chandler against April Chandler) and 19039141 (February 8, 2019, 10613 Birthstone, El Paso — criminal trespass contact with April Chandler at Daniel Chandler's residence, in which EPPD officers then transported April Chandler and three children to El Paso International Airport).

DUSM Hayes's activity logs, reports of contact, and case notes for January and February 2019 reflecting any contact with April Chandler (DOB February 28, 1974), Daniel Gary Chandler, or the parties' minor children.

Any record of DUSM Hayes's communication with EPPD Lieutenant Schneider of the Crimes Against Children unit during the same period.

Any follow-up record reflecting travel notifications to or from DUSM Hayes from April Chandler in the months following February 8, 2019 (per the EPPD narrative in 19039141, Ms. Chandler stated she "would be in contact with US Marshal Hayes regarding IO-01's arrest and would keep him posted on her travels").

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Wayne Whitney

Responsive records should include, without limitation:

CAD / dispatch logs and call-for-service records
Incident reports, supplemental reports, and narrative reports
Traffic-stop data records (including AZ DPS Form 2500 or equivalent)
NCIC, NLETS, and ACIC query logs showing warrant verification attempts
Body-worn camera and dash-camera footage
Audio dispatch recordings
Tow/impound records for any vehicle operated by Ms. Chandler
Records of any minor child (approx age 9 at the time) transported or released to child welfare authorities in connection with the stop
All communications between Arizona DPS personnel and the U.S. Marshals Service, specifically the USMS El Paso Division or Deputy U.S. Marshal Patrick Hayes, concerning April Chandler or a Texas arrest warrant issued against her, during the same period. This includes teletypes, NLETS messages, telephone call records, and dispatcher notes reflecting an inbound response from USMS that resulted in Ms. Chandler's release from custody.

Any records reflecting the existence, verification, hit-confirmation, or subsequent clearance of a Texas state warrant against April Chandler as queried by Arizona DPS dispatch or field personnel during the period specified.

Any records reflecting the release of April Chandler and her minor child on the shoulder or vicinity of an Arizona roadway following a warrant-verification call to USMS.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Wayne Whitney

title: Semi-annual PILOT statement/ Anduril Arsenal-1 Project Thor

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Semi-annual PILOT statement/ Anduril Arsenal-1 Project Thor
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all semi-annual PILOT Statements produced by the County Auditor or the Collection Agent that specifies that the amount due for the first or second half of the annual Project Thor PILOT payment to the Teays Valley Local School District and the Eastland-Fairfield Career and Technical Schools.

The statements are described in the Project Thor Compensation Agreement for the two schools: "On a semi-annual basis, between January 1 and March 1, and between April 1 and June 1, of each calendar year following each tax year of the CRA Exemption for each Building, the County Auditor or Collection Agent shall calculate the amount of the PILOT due in that calendar year to Teays Valley and Eastland-Fairfield from each Owner based on the formula outlined in Section 1(B) and on the square footage provided pursuant to Section 2(A), and reflect such PILOT in a written statement sent to each Owner and Teays Valley (the “PILOT Statements”). Each semi-annual PILOT Statement shall specify that the amount due is one-half of the PILOT for that year."

Additionally, I request copies of any objections by the Owner, Teays Valley, or Eastland-Fairfield to the calculation provided in writing to the County Auditor or Collection Agent after receipt of a PILOT Statement.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Taylor Barnes

title: Open Records Request — Communications Regarding Flock Safety Live Stream Access, March 2026

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request — Communications Regarding Flock Safety Live Stream Access, March 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All written communications — including emails, text messages, direct messages, platform notifications, and any other recorded communications — between any employee, officer, or agent of the City of Dunwoody or the Dunwoody Police Department and any employee or representative of Flock Safety, sent or received between February 23rd, 2026 and March 31, 2026, that reference, discuss, or relate to any of the following subjects:

1. Live stream or live view access to Dunwoody camera networks within the Flock Safety platform
2. The "Can View Live Stream For LPR" organization-level setting within the Flock Safety platform
3. Network sharing permissions, including but not limited to the canViewLiveStream permission, for any Dunwoody camera network
4. Any changes to network sharing settings, discoverability settings, or permission configurations within the Flock Safety platform
5. Preparation for, or discussion of, any presentation to the Dunwoody City Council regarding the Flock Safety platform

This request includes but is not limited to communications sent or received by Lt. Tim Fecht, Chief Carlson, City Manager Eric Linton, Technology Director Ginger LePage, or any other city or department personnel with administrative access to the Flock Safety platform.

Please provide all responsive records in their native electronic format where possible. If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please provide a written explanation identifying the specific statutory exemption claimed for each withheld record.

title: Escambia County Equestrian Center Land Use Agreements & Grant Contracts

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Escambia County Equestrian Center Land Use Agreements & Grant Contracts
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

To the Public Records Custodian:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law (Chapter 119, F.S.), I am requesting access to and copies of the following public records regarding the Escambia County Equestrian Center property (Parcel ID: 17-1S-31-2101-000-000; Situs: 7750 Mobile Hwy, Pensacola, FL 32526).

I am requesting any and all legal instruments, contracts, or agreements that place use-restrictions, conservation easements, or development limitations on this parcel. Specifically, I am seeking records generated or executed between October 11, 1999, and the present date, including:

Any recorded "Declaration of Restrictive Covenants" or "Conservation Easements" executed by Escambia County regarding this specific parcel.

Any grant award agreements, funding contracts, and accompanying "Notice of Limitation of Use" documents related to the development, construction, or maintenance of the Equestrian Center. This explicitly includes, but is not limited to, contracts with the Florida Communities Trust, the Land and Water Conservation Fund, or the Department of Environmental Protection that require the county to maintain the land for specific recreational or conservation uses for a set term of years.

Any interlocal or interagency agreements regarding the land-use viability or restriction of this specific parcel.

If you determine that any portion of these requested records is exempt from disclosure under Chapter 119, Florida law requires you to state in writing the basis for the exemption, including the exact statutory citation authorizing the redaction or denial.

Please produce all responsive records in a native electronic format (e.g., PDF) and transmit them via this digital portal to eliminate physical duplication fees.

If this request will incur a "Special Service Charge" for extensive clerical or supervisory assistance under §119.07(4)(d), please provide a detailed, itemized estimate of those costs prior to processing the request so we may work to narrow the scope if necessary.

Thank you for your lawful compliance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Matthew Joseph Luecht

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 2, 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting copies of the minutes for the Planning Board meeting held on March 2, 2026. This request is also made pursuant to the Massachusetts Open Meeting Law, M.G.L. c. 30A, §§ 18–25.

Please provide the minutes for this meeting.

If no minutes exist, please confirm that fact in writing.

This request includes any records that document the discussions, deliberations, and actions taken at the March 2, 2026 Planning Board meeting.

For context, I note that the Planning Board subsequently met on March 9, March 23, and April 6, 2026. During those meetings, it was stated on the record that no minutes were available. Under the Open Meeting Law, minutes are required to be created and approved in a timely manner, generally understood as within three meetings or 30 days, whichever is later. Based on these dates, the deadline for timely approval of the March 2, 2026 Planning Board meeting minutes would have been no later than the April 6, 2026 Planning Board meeting.

Under the Open Meeting Law, minutes of an open session are public records and must be made available upon request within 10 calendar days. If any portion of the requested records is withheld, please cite the specific legal basis for the denial.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eileen Kelleher

title: ICE FOIA Lapse in Appropriations Guidance, Policy, Instructions to Staff (Department of Homeland Security)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

On behalf of myself, Mami Chelo, The Law Library, and a coalition of media professionals and civil society requestors listed below, I write pursuant to 5 USC 552(a)(2) and 5 USC 552(a)(3) to seek copies of all policy guidance, instructions to staff, or similar records created by the Department of Homeland Security or its agency components, ICE and CBP, constituting the agency's official position as to the status of ICE and CBP FOIA offices during the lapse in federal appropriations that began in February 2025. 

Specifically, we seek records sufficient to demonstrate: 
(1) which personnel (by position title and total FTE or non-FTE personnel number) were furloughed from each subcomponent's FOIA office; 
(2) which personnel (by title and office) are currently responsible for handling FOIA responsibilities of the agency, including, but not limited to, (a) compliance with court-ordered deadlines and (b) tasking of FOIA requests and preservation notices in litigation; 
(3) which personnel are responsible for agency decision-making with respect to FOIA matters; 
(4) what functions each agency component's FOIA office and related FOIA counsel (ICE OPLA / CBP OCC) currently plays; 
(5) any guidance regarding settlement authority or decision-making and arrangement for payment of attorney fees and costs for which DHS FOIA agencies are responsible in FOIA litigation. 

An adequate search will include, but not be limited to, any official policy records or communications reflecting (a)(2) materials on which ICE Associate Director Kenneth Clark relied in the series of declarations he has filed in federal courts pertaining to the lapse in federal appropriations and its impact on ICE FOIA. 

I am a member of the media primarily engaged in information dissemination. I seek a fee waiver on that basis. See www.MuckRack.com/Andrew-Free. Mami Chelo and The Law Library are New York-based immigrants' rights organizations that obtain, analysis, synthesize, and dissemination up-to-date information about immigration law, policy, and practice to noncitizens, including people challenging their unlawful arrest and immigration detention, and families of people who died in ICE or CBP custody. 

Pursuant to 6 C.F.R. 5.5(e), we respectfully seek expedited processing of this request. I make the following statements under penalty of perjury pursuant to 28 USC 1746 in support of expedited processing, and declare that they are true and accurate to the best of my knowledge and understanding: 

1. Beginning in mid-February, ICE and CBP FOIA representatives and the U.S. Department of Justice announced to FOIA requestors and their counsel that the agency's FOIA office has been furloughed in light of the lapse in federal appropriations. 
2. Unlike in the 2025 government shutdown, DHS has published no guidance explaining the functions its agency subcomponents can or cannot perform, consistent with the Spending Clause and the Anti-Deficiency Act. 
3. There is presently no authoritative, government-disclosed policy, guideline, or instruction to staff explaining what DHS subcomponent FOIA Offices are legally permitted or prohibited from doing. 
4. In the absence of this legally mandatory publication, agency officials, including Mr. Kenneth Clark, have submitted declarations attesting to various staffing decisions and purported constraints on FOIA processing in light of the government shutdown. 
5. DHS, ICE, and CBP have been continued to engage in actions to inform the public regarding their activities during the lapse in federal appropriations through their various social media channels, public affairs offices, and websites. 
6. There is an urgent need to inform the public about the agency's internal policy instructions with respect to FOIA compliance during the present government shutdown. 
7. As requestors, we have a procedural due-process right to lawful, regular processing of our FOIA requests in compliance with the language of the statute. 
8. Currently, the failure of DHS, ICE, and CBP to affirmatively disclose its FOIA shutdown guidance deprives us of that right by rendering secret and unknowable the agency's current FOIA guidelines. 
9. The absence of public, affirmatively posted policy guidance and instructions to staff from DHS and its subcomponents' FOIA offices has occasioned needless, inefficient, and time-consuming litigation and motions practice to enforce FOIA litigants' requests across the country. See, e.g., https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/28035323-36-opinion-on-motion-to-compel/. 
10. We will immediately make these records available to the public upon receipt.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrew Free

title: Public records request for Canal Boulevard / Homedale Street crash on January 6, 2016

communications.subject: Louisiana Public Records Law Request: Public records request for Canal Boulevard / Homedale Street crash on January 6, 2016
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Louisiana Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Louisiana Public Records Law, I am requesting copies of all records in the possession, custody, or control of the New Orleans Fire Department relating to the serious traffic accident at or near the intersection of Canal Boulevard and Homedale Street in New Orleans on the evening of January 6, 2016, reported by NOLA.com the following morning under the headline “At least 2 injured in serious accident on Canal Boulevard.” - The report is short and lacks most critical information. A link is provided here: https://www.nola.com/news/traffic/at-least-2-injured-in-serious-accident-on-canal-boulevard/article_35f4a3cd-84d0-501a-a0f0-3105152b3c1e.html

This request includes, but is not limited to:

    fire or rescue incident reports
    unit run reports
    dispatch and response logs
    call sheets
    scene logs
    rescue or extrication records
    supervisor reports
    photographs taken at the scene
    audio, video, or other media depicting the scene, vehicles, or occupants
    any other releasable records relating to the department’s response to this incident

Please construe this request broadly to include records indexed by location, date, time, incident number, dispatch number, or any other identifier. If the exact date or time is recorded slightly differently, please include records for the crash described in the article above, including records from the late evening of January 6, 2016 and early hours of January 7, 2016 if necessary to capture the incident.

I am also specifically requesting records sufficient to identify or describe the occupants of the vehicle or vehicles involved, if such information appears in department records. This includes, if available in releasable form:

    names of occupants
    age or approximate age
    sex
    number of occupants
    seating position or driver/passenger status, if noted
    general condition or injury status as reflected in fire department records
    whether any occupant was trapped, extricated, transported, or pronounced deceased at scene

If names or other identifying information are exempt from release, please do not withhold the record in full. Instead, please provide the record with only the exempt information redacted and release all non-exempt identifying or descriptive information.

If any requested audio, video, or photographs once existed but are no longer retained, please provide any record showing that fact, including any retention, deletion, purge, or disposition information sufficient to show whether such material was created and what became of it.

If any portion of this request is denied, please provide:

    the specific basis for the denial or redaction, and
    the non-exempt portions of the responsive records.

If no responsive records are found, please state that explicitly and identify, to the extent possible, the record systems or locations searched.

I prefer records in electronic form if available.

Please do not incur fees greater than $20 without contacting me first for approval. If the cost will exceed that amount, please do not continue processing the request until I approve it with an estimate. I understand estimates may not be the final price, the estimated price should be no more than 3x the base / $60.

Thank you for your time.

Sincerely,
Requester
via MuckRock

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

LARECS

title: Law Enforcement Disability & ADA Training Records

communications.subject: Arkansas Freedom of Information Act Request: Law Enforcement Disability & ADA Training Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Arkansas Department of Public Safety

Subject: FOIA Request – Law Enforcement Disability & ADA Training Records

To: Arkansas Department of Public Safety

Dear FOIA Officer:

Pursuant to the Arkansas Freedom of Information Act, I am requesting access to and copies of all records, reports, training materials, curricula, and related documents regarding the training of law enforcement officers on interactions with persons with disabilities as mandated by Arkansas Code 12-9-116. This includes, but is not limited to:

Training records and reports from the Arkansas Law Enforcement Training Academy (ALETA) related to disability awareness, ADA compliance, effective communication, and crisis intervention.

Materials regarding the “Just Policing” program and any specialized “Law Enforcement and Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA)” courses.

Documents pertaining to the Blue Envelope Initiative and any other programs designed to improve interactions between drivers with autism or mental illnesses and police.

All correspondence, internal memos, or guidance documents relating to the above training and programs.

Any other documents, emails, or reports relating to law enforcement training on disability awareness and ADA obligations.

If possible, please provide these records in electronic format. If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me in advance.

Thank you for your attention to this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Christopher Reed

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: LAPD 1033/1208 Program Transfer Records (1996–1998)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I respectfully request copies of the following records maintained by the Defense Logistics Agency, DLA Disposition Services (formerly the Defense Reutilization and Marketing Service, DRMS/DRMO), and any predecessor offices that administered the 1208 Program and its successor 1033 Program:

1/ The complete application(s), Memorandum of Agreement, State Plan of Operation, and any signed participation agreements submitted by or on behalf of the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) to participate in the 1208 Program (10 U.S.C. § 2576, Pub. L. 101-189) and/or the 1033 Program (10 U.S.C. § 2576a, Pub. L. 104-201) between January 1, 1996 and December 31, 1998.
2/ All transfer records, requisition forms (including DD Form 1348-1A or equivalent), shipping documents, property accountability records, and inventory lists documenting the transfer of M16 rifles (NSN 1005-00-073-9421 or related national stock numbers) from any Department of Defense source to LAPD during that period — including but not limited to the approximately 600 M16 rifles reported to have been transferred to LAPD following the February 28, 1997 North Hollywood bank shootout.
3/ Records identifying the originating military installation(s) for the transferred rifles, including any documentation showing shipment from the Anniston Army Depot (Anniston, Alabama) or other Army depots.
4/ Any correspondence, memoranda, or email between DLA/DRMS/DRMO personnel and LAPD, the California State Coordinator for the 1033 Program, or the U.S. Army concerning the LAPD rifle transfer(s) during the above period.

Format: Electronic copies (PDF) via email are preferred.

Fee waiver: I request a fee waiver under 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii) because disclosure is in the public interest, concerning the operations of government, specifically the militarization of civilian policing and the federal transfer of military weapons to local law enforcement. This request is not for commercial use. In the alternative, please notify me before incurring fees exceeding $25.

Expedited processing: Not requested.

Please segregate and release any non-exempt portions of responsive records and cite the specific exemption(s) relied upon for any withholding. If responsive records are maintained by another DoD component (e.g., U.S. Army TACOM, Anniston Army Depot, or the Army's Law Enforcement Support Office), please refer this request accordingly and notify me of the referral.

Sincerely,
Daniel Brown

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Brown
